Themes

DuVay ships with light, dark, auto, and high-contrast themes. Switch by setting data-w-theme on the root element.

Switching themes

Set the attribute manually on the html element, or call the theme API exposed by duvay.js:

<html data-w-theme="dark">

// or programmatically:
WTheme.set('dark')          // light, dark, auto, high-contrast
WTheme.cycle()              // cycles light to dark to auto
WTheme.get()                // returns current theme

Auto mode

The auto theme follows the user's operating-system preference via prefers-color-scheme. Falls back to light when no preference is set.

High-contrast

For accessibility-first surfaces, the high-contrast theme strengthens borders and uses near-pure foreground/background pairs that meet WCAG AAA contrast on text.

Tokens

Every theme defines the same semantic tokens. Components only ever reference the token name, never the underlying hex value.

Color tokens

--w-primary, --w-on-primary, --w-primary-container, --w-on-primary-container
--w-surface, --w-surface-container-low, --w-surface-container-high
--w-text, --w-text-subtle, --w-text-very-subtle
--w-border, --w-divider, --w-hover, --w-selected, --w-selected-text
--w-error, --w-success, --w-warning      (each has a -container / on-container pair)
--w-inverse-surface, --w-inverse-on-surface, --w-inverse-primary
--w-scrim, --w-shadow-color

Spacing

Both a numeric scale (--w-space-1 through --w-space-20, 4 px steps) and a t-shirt scale (xs, sm, md, lg, xl, 2xl, 3xl) are exposed. Pick either — the t-shirt names are aliases.

Shape

--w-radius-sm, --w-radius, --w-radius-md, --w-radius-lg, --w-radius-xl, --w-radius-pill.

Custom theme

Override any token under your own attribute or class — it cascades through the whole framework. Example pattern:

[data-w-theme="brand"] {
  --w-primary: #5a31f4;
  --w-on-primary: #ffffff;
  --w-primary-container: #ebe4ff;
  --w-on-primary-container: #1b0654;
}
